title: carex atrata subsp. aterrima
intro: Carex atrata subsp. aterrima is a subspecies of Carex atrata, commonly known as the Black Tussock Sedge, a type of flowering plant in the family Cyperaceae. This plant is used in traditional medicine, particularly in Europe, for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Carex atrata subsp. aterrima include treating wounds, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and used as an infusion or decoction, often combined with other herbs to create a medicinal tea or tincture.
